# Multiple Barcode / QR Code Generator

> A free, browser-based tool to generate many barcodes (CODE128) and QR codes at once. Paste a list of values — one per line — and instantly get a tidy grid of codes. No sign-up, no installation, no server: every code is generated locally in the browser, so the data you type never leaves your device. Download each code as PNG, download all as a ZIP, or print the whole grid (and "Save as PDF").

Live app: https://amebahead.github.io/barcodegen/

## What it does
- Bulk / batch generation: turn a list of strings into multiple barcodes or QR codes in one step.
- Two code types, switchable with one toggle: CODE128 barcode (auto subset) and QR code.
- Responsive grid layout that auto-fits the screen, with the original value shown under each code.
- Per-cell error handling: invalid values fail only in their own cell; the rest still render.
- Export: individual PNG per code, "Download all" as a ZIP of PNGs, and Print (browser "Save as PDF").

## Who it is for
Small-business owners, online sellers, warehouse and inventory staff, retail, event organizers, makers, and developers who need to create many labels or codes quickly and for free.

## Key facts
- Price: free (no account, no ads-wall).
- Privacy: fully client-side; input values are not transmitted to any server. Anonymous visit counts are collected via GoatCounter.
- Tech: TypeScript + Vite, JsBarcode (CODE128), qrcode-generator (QR), output as SVG.
- Languages of guidance on the page: English, Korean (한국어), Japanese (日本語), Chinese (中文).

## Common questions
- How do I generate multiple barcodes at once? Paste one value per line into the text box; each line becomes its own code in the grid.
- Is it free? Yes, completely free with no sign-up.
- Which barcode format? CODE128 (supports ASCII letters, digits, and symbols). Use QR mode for non-ASCII text such as Korean, Japanese, or Chinese, and for URLs.
- Can I download or print? Yes — PNG per code, all as a ZIP, or print/Save-as-PDF.
- Does it work offline / is my data private? Codes are generated in your browser; the values you enter are never sent to a server.

## Keywords
